post.description: Creates a new business vault, optionally initializing with the provided data

parameters.name: x-idempotency-id
parameters.description: To safely support retrying requests without accidentally performing the same operation multiple times, provide a client-generated `x-idempotency-id`. Requests made with the same `x-idempotency-id` value will no-op and return the same result. Note, if `x-idempotency-id` is provided, initial data may not be specified in the HTTP body.

parameters.name: x-sandbox-id
parameters.description: When provided, creates a sandbox user with the provided sandbox ID. Sandbox IDs allow you to create multiple users with the same contact info. In order to log in using an existing sandbox user, you can provide its Sandbox ID in the Footprint flow.

parameters.name: x-external-id
parameters.description: To support attaching external identifiers (like a foreign user id) to this request, provide a client-generated `x-external-id`. Requests made with the same `x-external-id` value will no-op and return the same result. `x-external-id` must be globally unique to your organization. Note, if `x-external-id` is provided, initial data may not be specified in the HTTP body.

requestBody.description: Key-value map of data to add to the vault. For more documentation on available keys, see [here](https://docs.onefootprint.com/vault/fields).

get.description: View basic details for a business. See the APIs under `/businesses/{fp_bid}/vault` for information on reading vault data.
Returns the list of fields present on the vault.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
Optionally, a comma-separated list of fields whose existence to check. For example,
 `id.first_name,id.ssn4,custom.bank_account`

delete.description: Deletes data in a business vault.

patch.description: Updates data in a business vault.

post.description: Decrypts the specified list of fields from the provided vault.
Checks if provided data is valid before adding it to the vault.
Validate a short-lived onboarding session token and exchange it for a long-lived fp_id
Creates a new user, optionally initializing with the provided data

parameters.name: x-idempotency-id
parameters.description: To safely support retrying requests without accidentally performing the same operation multiple times, provide a client-generated `x-idempotency-id`. Requests made with the same `x-idempotency-id` value will no-op and return the same result. Note, if `x-idempotency-id` is provided, initial data may not be specified in the HTTP body.

parameters.name: x-sandbox-id
parameters.description: When provided, creates a sandbox user with the provided sandbox ID. Sandbox IDs allow you to create multiple users with the same contact info. In order to log in using an existing sandbox user, you can provide its Sandbox ID in the Footprint flow.

parameters.name: x-external-id
parameters.description: To support attaching external identifiers (like a foreign user id) to this request, provide a client-generated `x-external-id`. Requests made with the same `x-external-id` value will no-op and return the same result. `x-external-id` must be globally unique to your organization. Note, if `x-external-id` is provided, initial data may not be specified in the HTTP body.

requestBody.description: Key-value map of data to add to the vault. For more documentation on available keys, see [here](https://docs.onefootprint.com/vault/fields).

patch.description: Updates data in a vault given a short-lived, entity-scoped client token.

post.description: Decrypts the specified list of fields given a short-lived, entity-scoped client token

get.description: Decrypts and downloads the user's data as specified by the provided token.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: Short-lived client token with `decrypt_download` permissions to download a single piece of vaulted data.

post.description: Checks if provided data is valid before adding it to the vault given a short-lived, user-scoped client token
Updates data in a vault given a short-lived, entity-scoped client token.

parameters.name: content-type
parameters.description: Specify the content type of the object like `application/json` or `image/png`

get.description: View basic details for a user. See the APIs under `/users/{fp_bid}/vault` for information on reading vault data.

post.description: Create a short-lived token safe to pass to your client for operations to vault or decrypt data for this user. This API is often used in combination with the [Footprint Form SDK](https://docs.onefootprint.com/embedded-components/getting-started).

Create an identified token for the provided fp_id. This token may be passed into Footprint.js's KYC and KYB SDKs to bootstrap a user's onboarding with known information. Re-auth will be required with Footprint. More detailed documentation can be found [here](https://docs.onefootprint.com/integrate/user-specific-sessions).

get.description: Returns the list of fields present on the vault.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
Optionally, a comma-separated list of fields whose existence to check. For example,
 `id.first_name,id.ssn4,custom.bank_account`

delete.description: Deletes data in a user vault.

patch.description: Updates data in a user vault.

post.description: Decrypts the specified list of fields from the provided vault.
Checks if provided data is valid before adding it to the vault.
Updates data in a user vault.

parameters.name: content-type
parameters.description: Specify the content type of the object like `application/json` or `image/png`

post.description: Invoke the vault proxy 'just-in-time' (JIT) to securely send and receive data to a target destination. Please contact support to enable this API.

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-target-url
parameters.description: Target proxy destination URL Required if "just-in-time" OR ignored if using proxy configuration

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-method
parameters.description: HTTP Method VERB for the proxy destination request (defaults to POST)

parameters.name: x-fp-path-and-query
parameters.description: Egress destination URL path and query string to append

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-ingress-content-type
parameters.description: Content-type for the proxy ingress

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-access-reason
parameters.description: Access reason for any egress decryption operations during the proxy request

parameters.name: x-fp-id
parameters.description: When proxy requests are on behalf of a single footprint vault, you can can omit the `fp_id_` prefix on token identifiers, and just use `id.x` or `custom.y` instead of `fp_id_xyz.id.x` or `fp_id_xyz.custom.y`.  Similarly, if specifying proxy configuration ingress rules from a stored configuration the corresponding token must be assigned just-in-time via a headers  i.e: `x-fp-id: fp_id_abc`

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-ingress-rule
parameters.description: Configure one more ingress rules  x-fp-proxy-ingress-rule: fp_id_abc.custom.credit_card_number=$.data.card.number x-fp-proxy-ingress-rule: fp_id_abc.custom.credit_card_exp=$.data.card.expiration x-fp-proxy-ingress-rule: fp_id_abc.custom.credit_card_cvc=$.data.card.security_code

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-fwd-
parameters.description: Prefixes custom headers to forward along egress For example `x-fp-proxy-fwd-MYHEADER: hello world` sends `MYHEADER: hello world` to the destination

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-client-cert
parameters.description: Base64 encoded PEM client certificate to use (required if using key)

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-client-key
parameters.description: Base64 encoded PEM client key to use (required if using cert)

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-pin-cert
parameters.description: Configure one or more base64 encoded PEM server certificates to validate and pin proxy destination TLS connections.

post.description: Invoke the vault proxy by configuration id to securely send and receive data to a target destination. Please contact support to enable this API.

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-method
parameters.description: HTTP Method VERB for the proxy destination request (defaults to POST)

parameters.name: x-fp-path-and-query
parameters.description: Egress destination URL path and query string to append

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-ingress-content-type
parameters.description: Content-type for the proxy ingress

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-access-reason
parameters.description: Access reason for any egress decryption operations during the proxy request

parameters.name: x-fp-id
parameters.description: When proxy requests are on behalf of a single footprint vault, you can can omit the `fp_id_` prefix on token identifiers, and just use `id.x` or `custom.y` instead of `fp_id_xyz.id.x` or `fp_id_xyz.custom.y`.  Similarly, if specifying proxy configuration ingress rules from a stored configuration the corresponding token must be assigned just-in-time via a headers  i.e: `x-fp-id: fp_id_abc`

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-ingress-rule
parameters.description: Configure one more ingress rules  x-fp-proxy-ingress-rule: fp_id_abc.custom.credit_card_number=$.data.card.number x-fp-proxy-ingress-rule: fp_id_abc.custom.credit_card_exp=$.data.card.expiration x-fp-proxy-ingress-rule: fp_id_abc.custom.credit_card_cvc=$.data.card.security_code

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-fwd-
parameters.description: Prefixes custom headers to forward along egress For example `x-fp-proxy-fwd-MYHEADER: hello world` sends `MYHEADER: hello world` to the destination

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-client-cert
parameters.description: Base64 encoded PEM client certificate to use (required if using key)

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-client-key
parameters.description: Base64 encoded PEM client key to use (required if using cert)

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-pin-cert
parameters.description: Configure one or more base64 encoded PEM server certificates to validate and pin proxy destination TLS connections.

Client Token.name: X-Fp-Authorization
Client Token.description: Short-lived client token to perform actions for a given user.

Dashboard Token.name: X-Fp-Dashboard-Authorization
Dashboard Token.description: Short-lived token for an authenticated dashboard user.

Secret API Key.name: X-Footprint-Secret-Key
Secret API Key.description: Secret API key. You can create and view your API keys in the dashboard. This key should never be sent to your client and should be treated as a secret on your server.

fields.description:
List of data identifiers to decrypt. For example, `id.first_name`, `id.ssn4`,
 `custom.bank_account`

items.description:
Represents a piece of data stored inside the user vault.
 Mostly used in requests to decrypt a piece of data and in access events to show the log of decrypted items.

reason.description:
Reason for the data decryption. This will be logged.
 The reason must be provided either here or in the client token

transforms.description:
A list of filter and transform functions to apply to each decrypted datum.
 Omit or leave empty to apply no transforms
 Can find more information on allowed transform functions on our docs

items.description: Represents a data transform to apply to underlying plaintext behind a data identifier. Specify only one of the following fields. See more here: https://docs.onefootprint.com/vault/proxy#filter-functions

decrypt_reason.description: If the token is allowed to decrypt, provide a default decryption reason

fields.description:
List of data identifiers to which this token will have access. For example, `id.first_name`,
 `id.ssn4`, `custom.bank_account`.. Should not be specified when using the `vault_card`
 scope.

items.description:
Represents a piece of data stored inside the user vault.
 Mostly used in requests to decrypt a piece of data and in access events to show the log of decrypted items.

scope.description:
Specify the permissions of this token.
 - `vault` allows writing to the specified fields.
 - `decrypt` allows decrypting the specified fields.
 - `vault_and_decrypt` allows both.
 - `decrypt_download` allows decrypting a single piece of data as a file.
 - `vault_card` is a shorthand to generate a token to vault a card with a random alias.

ttl.description:
Time to live until this token expires, provided in seconds. Defaults to 30 minutes. Must be
 at least 60 seconds, at most 1 day

expires_at.description: The time at which the token expires

fields.description: The fields that this token has permissions to operate on, according to the requested scope.

items.description:
Represents a piece of data stored inside the user vault.
 Mostly used in requests to decrypt a piece of data and in access events to show the log of decrypted items.

token.description: The short-lived token that gives temporary access to perform operations for this user

fp_bid.description:
The existing business to be linked to this user's onboarding session. Only takes effect when
 onboarding onto a KYB playbook.
 If no `fp_bid` is provided here when onboarding onto a KYB playbook, the user will be asked
 to enter their business information and create a new business.

key.description:
Can only be provided when the kind is `onboard`.
 Optionally, the publishable key of the playbook onto which you would like this user to
 onboard. The user will be asked to provide any missing information required by playbook. If
 you provide the key here, you can omit providing it in the frontend Footprint.js SDK
 integration.

kind.description:
The kind of token to create.
 - `onboard` creates a token that onboards the user onto a specific playbook, specified by
   the `key`.
 - `reonboard` creates a token that reonboards the user onto the last playbook that they
   onboarded onto.
 - `inherit` creates a token that inherits any operation previously requested via the
   dashboard.
 - `user` simply create a token for the user. A playbook key may be provided directly to the
   Footprint Verify SDK to trigger onboarding.
 - `update_auth_methods` creates a token that allows the user to update their contact info
   using the Footprint Auth SDK.
 You can find more information on the options [here](https://docs.onefootprint.com/integrate/user-specific-sessions#step-2-generate-an-auth-token-for-the-user-on-your-backend-token-kinds).

limit_auth_methods.description:
Can only be provided when the kind is `update_auth_methods`. The set of auth methods that
 you would like to be allowed to be updated. When not provided, the token allows updating any
 auth method.

ttl_min.description:
Time to live until this token expires, provided in minutes. Defaults to 60 minutes. Must be
 at least 1 minute, at most 1 day

expires_at.description: The time at which the token (and link) expire

link.description:
A Footprint link embedding the `token` that can be sent to this user to allow them to
 complete the flow. This is useful to send in an automated message to the end user.

token.description:
A short-lived token that can be passed into the Verify SDK to allow the user to complete the
 flow. This is useful when you'd like to open a native interface inside your app for the user
 to complete the flow.

sandbox_id.description: Only populated for users created in sandbox mode.

next.description: The `cursor` parameter to provide in order to request the next page of results.

fields.description:
List of data identifiers to decrypt. For example, `id.first_name`, `id.ssn4`,
 `custom.bank_account`

items.description:
Represents a piece of data stored inside the user vault.
 Mostly used in requests to decrypt a piece of data and in access events to show the log of decrypted items.

reason.description: Reason for the data decryption. This will be logged

transforms.description:
A list of filter and transform functions to apply to each decrypted datum.
 Omit or leave empty to apply no transforms.
 Can find more information on allowed transform functions on our docs

items.description: Represents a data transform to apply to underlying plaintext behind a data identifier. Specify only one of the following fields. See more here: https://docs.onefootprint.com/vault/proxy#filter-functions

DecryptResponse.description: A key-value map with the corresponding decrypted values

delete_all.description: When true, deletes all data in the vault.

fields.description:
List of data identifiers to delete. For example, `id.first_name`, `id.ssn4`,
 `custom.bank_account`

items.description:
Represents a piece of data stored inside the user vault.
 Mostly used in requests to decrypt a piece of data and in access events to show the log of decrypted items.

DeleteVaultResponse.description: A key-value map of identifier to whether the identifier was successfully deleted in the vault

GetVaultResponse.description: A key-value map of identifier to whether the identifier exists in the vault

sandbox_id.description: Only populated for users created in sandbox mode.

requires_additional_info.description:
When non-null, there is additional info pending collection from this user. In this case, you
 may create a token for this user with the `inherit` operation. This token can be used to
 collect any outstanding information.

note.description:
The human-readable note you provided in the dashboard when requesting additional info. You
 may choose to render this message in your own application.

timestamp.description: The timestamp at which the additional info was requested.

validation_token.description: An cryptographically generated auth token to authenticate a session

business.description: Information on the business and its onboarding session. Provided for KYB playbook sessions

user.description:
Information on the user and their onboarding session. Provided for KYC and KYB playbook
 sessions

user_auth.description: Information on the authenticated user and the auth method they used
